clusters.root_cause_summary: The downstairs stair centroid lies in a navmesh-disconnected island on both scenes; pathfinder.find_path() never succeeds regardless of which centroid is selected or how scoring is adjusted, so floor_step never resets and the couch floor is never entered. No DP parameter, scoring patch, or stair-management lever can generate navmesh connectivity that the simulator does not provide.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical: both show ≥2 consecutive Reach_stair_centroid=False cycles per attempt, no floor_step reset across any tested candidate, and terminal failure via frontier exhaustion. Behavioral fingerprint is locked across 9+ candidates per scene. Different: q3zU7Yy5E5s exhausts at step 381 with a single stable centroid [-2.12, 3.27]; qyAac8rV8Zk exhausts at steps 239–243 with centroid shifting ~0.04m under RSD but remaining equally disconnected. Both goals are couches on the lower floor, suggesting a structural pattern in HM3D couch placement relative to navmesh islands.
clusters.transfer_notes: candidate_35's RSD discount on qyAac8rV8Zk perturbed the centroid by 0.04m and added 4 steps of approach geometry but produced identical Reach_stair_centroid=False, confirming that any frontier-score manipulation that merely selects among geometrically equivalent disconnected centroids is a strict no-op. candidate_5 broke the dominant fingerprint on both scenes by extending floor-1 exploration before stair entry, yet still failed — ruling out timing as a confound and confirming the disconnection is stable across all agent positions. The passive climb_stair path that rescued mL8ThkuaVTM (candidate_0, steps 91-102) is the key transfer insight: if the agent can physically approach the stair region during normal explore without requiring pathfinder connectivity to a centroid, a passive upstairs-style trigger could bypass get_close_to_stair entirely.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: passive_floor_detection_triggering_climb_stair_without_get_close_to_stair_prerequisite: monitor stair-pixel density in the current RGB frame during explore mode and trigger climb_stair directly (as candidate_0 does on mL8ThkuaVTM) without requiring pathfinder connectivity to a centroid. Complementary gate: habitat_navmesh_island_radius_query at the stair centroid before entering get_close_to_stair — if the centroid's island radius is below a threshold (e.g., <0.5m), skip the centroid-approach path entirely and rely on passive visual detection.

clusters.root_cause_summary: candidate_2's hysteresis patch rerouted the agent from the passive climb_stair path (successful in candidate_0 at steps 91–102, floor_step reset at step 287) into a get_close_to_stair→look_for_downstair oscillation that inflated floor_reinits from 3 to 6 and exhausted the episode budget. The toilet is 2D-connected to the agent start (failure tag: 'feasible'); the regression is entirely patch-induced.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single scene. The defining asymmetry: candidate_0 reached the toilet by passive stair detection during explore mode without ever entering get_close_to_stair; every candidate that introduces any stair-approach modification (hysteresis or otherwise) disrupts this path and fails. The 'feasible' classifier tag is the key differentiator from cluster pre_climb_navmesh_disconnect — the geometry is solvable, not the navmesh.
clusters.transfer_notes: This scene is the functional inverse of q3zU7Yy5E5s/qyAac8rV8Zk: those scenes need an alternative to get_close_to_stair because their centroid is navmesh-disconnected; this scene must PRESERVE the passive climb_stair path that bypasses get_close_to_stair. Any passive floor detection fix designed for cluster pre_climb_navmesh_disconnect must not add a guard that delays or suppresses passive stair triggers during explore mode, or it will reproduce this regression on mL8ThkuaVTM.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: revert_hysteresis_patch unconditionally, then add an explicit guard: if passive climb_stair is triggered during explore mode, suppress all get_close_to_stair interceptors for the remainder of that floor transition. This ensures no future stair-approach patch can divert an already-committed passive climb.

clusters.root_cause_summary: candidate_35's room-scale saturation discount (RSD) reduced the raw BLIP-2 score of the TV-containing frontier from 0.107 to below the navigate trigger threshold 19 steps before the reliable navigate transition seen in 12/13 prior candidates, deflecting the agent into a 100-step detour and driving all zones to DENSITY_FLOOR with no recovery. This is a pure regression on a scene solved 12/13 times prior; no floor transition or navmesh issue is involved.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single scene. The mechanism is a missing lower bound in RSD: any frontier with raw BLIP-2 score just above background (0.10–0.15) that coincidentally occupies a high-density zone will be suppressed below action thresholds even when it is the globally correct target. The TV frontier at 0.107 sat in this vulnerable band. The 2 LLM invocations in candidate_35 vs 0 in successful runs confirm the divergent episode required LLM arbitration unavailable in the baseline path.
clusters.transfer_notes: The same RSD mechanism caused a centroid shift on qyAac8rV8Zk (cluster pre_climb_navmesh_disconnect) without fixing disconnection — together, these two scenes establish that RSD introduces score-surface perturbations that are harmful to single-floor reliable successes and orthogonal to navmesh failures. RSD must never be deployed without a high-value frontier preservation guard (raw score threshold ≥0.10). The density-update-only-in-explore-mode variant would also prevent the late-episode score collapse observed here.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: RSD_score_floor_protection: before applying the density multiplier to any frontier, check if its raw BLIP-2 score exceeds a preservation threshold (e.g., 0.10); if so, clamp the multiplier to a minimum of 1.0 (no discount). This directly targets the failure mode: the TV frontier at 0.107 would have been preserved while low-value repeated frontiers would still be suppressed. An alternative with similar effect: disable RSD entirely before the first floor_reinit fires, since the first-floor approach is where the reliable TV signal appears.

clusters.root_cause_summary: Stairs are physically traversed (floor_step resets at step 198), but floor 2 contains only 2 frontiers in a navmesh-disconnected component that excludes the bed room; the agent exhausts floor-2 frontiers in ~16 steps and terminates via T4_NOQUIT with an empty pool. No frontier scoring, timing, or DP lever can expand a navmesh-capped frontier pool of 2.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single scene. Structurally related to cluster pre_climb_navmesh_disconnect (both involve navmesh islands isolating the goal), but the failure point differs: here the stair IS climbed successfully, and the disconnection is on the destination floor rather than in the stair centroid. This makes it strictly harder to address — the stair traversal succeeds, but the post-climb exploration space is structurally empty. Identical behavioral fingerprint across all 14 tested candidates, including candidate_35 with confirmed-active RSD discount on floor 2.
clusters.transfer_notes: candidate_35's RSD discount was confirmed active on floor 2 (0.9m frontier score 0.107→0.021) yet produced zero behavioral change — the most direct proof that frontier scoring is irrelevant when the frontier pool is navmesh-capped at 2. The passive_stair_detection lever proposed for cluster pre_climb_navmesh_disconnect does NOT transfer here (stair is already climbed). The only structurally distinct untested interventions involve post-climb navmesh queries or frontier seeding via geometry rather than semantic scoring.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: navmesh_subgraph_connectivity_check_before_floor2_exploration: after climbing stairs, query pathfinder.island_radius() from the landing point; if it is below a threshold indicating a small disconnected stub, trigger frontier_seeding_beyond_stair_landing using 3D raycast from the stair landing toward room-boundary candidates to populate frontiers in the connected goal subgraph. Fallback: downstairs_fallback_to_retry_different_floor_entry_point_after_floor2_exhaustion — when floor-2 frontier pool empties in <20 floor_steps after stair climb, attempt a second stair approach from an alternative heading.

forbidden_moves.reason: Globally forbidden across all scenes per standing protocol.

Confirmed no-op on q3zU7Yy5E5s (identical fingerprint, same centroid); shifted centroid by 0.04m on qyAac8rV8Zk but produced identical Reach_stair_centroid=False. Cannot generate navmesh connectivity; any score perturbation selects among equally disconnected centroids.
Confirmed active on floor 2 (score 0.107→0.021) yet zero behavioral change; frontier pool is navmesh-capped at 2, making scoring irrelevant to exploration coverage.
This mechanism IS the root cause; reapplying it without a score-floor preservation guard will reproduce the TV frontier suppression and 100-step detour.
Directly caused the regression: diverted passive climb_stair path into get_close_to_stair→look_for_downstair oscillation, inflating floor_reinits from 3 to 6.
Ruled out on both q3zU7Yy5E5s and qyAac8rV8Zk; altering hysteresis cannot bridge a navmesh-disconnected centroid.
All 12 DP parameters are confirmed orthogonal to navmesh disconnection across both scenes; the failure is structural, not parametric.
Tested and confirmed no-op on both scenes; detecting disconnection early does not provide an alternative traversal route — it only causes earlier termination without finding the goal.
Blacklisting the disconnected centroid leaves no alternative stair entry; the entire accessible stair region on both scenes is navmesh-disconnected, so no centroid substitution resolves the failure.

Tested on both scenes (candidate_13); blocking stair re-entry after T failures causes earlier termination but does not generate an alternative floor-entry path — net effect is fewer wasted steps, not a success.
Confirmed no-op on both scenes (candidate_32); BLIP-2 gradient detection operates on frontier scoring and is entirely orthogonal to stair centroid navmesh connectivity.
